meta.title: BUILD & RUN
meta.subtitle: 6'0" · 67 kg · Beginner · Strength + Cardio · Starting weights included
meta.footer: RUN THE NEIGHBORHOOD · LIFT THE DUMBBELLS · BUILD THE BODY

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.note: Keep chest up, controlled descent

exercises.name: Pull Ups
exercises.note: Best back builder. Overhand grip

exercises.name: Dumbbell Overhead Press
exercises.note: Seated or standing. Keep core braced

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: One arm at a time, brace on a chair

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Light weight, slow and controlled

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Alternate arms, full range of motion

exercises.name: Dumbbell Tricep Kickback
exercises.note: Keep upper arm parallel to floor

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.note: Hold one DB at chest. Go deep

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Push hips back, feel the hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunges
exercises.note: Long stride, keep torso upright

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Ups
exercises.note: Sturdy chair — drive through the heel

exercises.name: Dumbbell Sumo Squat
exercises.note: Wide stance, toes out — hits inner thighs

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.note: Hold DBs for weight, pause 1 sec at top

cardio.pace: Conversational — you should be able to hold a full sentence while running
cardio.zoneDesc: Low intensity fat-burning zone. Builds aerobic base without hurting recovery.

steps.detail: Brisk walk to prime the legs
Slow, steady pace — never gasping for air
Gradually slow to a walk, don't stop abruptly

exercises.name: Gada 360s
exercises.note: Full circular swing overhead. Master the skill first

exercises.name: Gada 10-to-2 Swings
exercises.note: Side swings from 10 o'clock to 2 o'clock position

exercises.name: Gada Front & Back Swings
exercises.note: Pendulum motion — great for shoulder mobility

exercises.name: Full Body Stretch
exercises.note: Hips, chest, hamstrings, lats

exercises.name: Chin Ups (Underhand)
exercises.note: Underhand = more bicep involvement than pull ups

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Prop bench up or use an angled surface

exercises.name: Dumbbell Renegade Row
exercises.note: Push-up position, row one DB. Core stays tight

exercises.name: Dumbbell Arnold Press
exercises.note: Rotate palms-in to palms-out as you press up

exercises.name: Gada Halo
exercises.note: Circle around your head — great for shoulder health

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
exercises.note: Neutral grip — hits brachialis for arm thickness

exercises.name: Diamond Push Ups
exercises.note: Hands in diamond shape — tricep burnout finisher

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.note: Balance on one leg — great glute + hamstring builder

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Shoulders on couch, DB on hips, squeeze glutes at top

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: Rear foot on chair. Start BW — it will humble you

exercises.name: Glute Bridge March
exercises.note: In bridge, alternate lifting each knee

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.note: Go slow — one of the best core exercises in existence

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.note: From pull up bar — straight legs for max difficulty

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.note: Sit on chair, DB on knees, rise on balls of feet

cardio.pace: Slightly challenging — breathing harder but still in control
cardio.when: Morning — best time for fat burning and energy levels
cardio.zoneDesc: Builds cardiovascular endurance. Your weekly long run — the backbone of your cardio fitness.

steps.detail: Brisk walk, light leg swings and hip circles
Slow jog — build the base, don't rush
Walk it out, then stretch calves, quads, hips

exercises.name: Meal prep for the week
exercises.note: Cook rice, chicken, eggs in bulk

exercises.name: Track your weight
exercises.note: Weigh yourself every morning, same time

exercises.name: Review progress & adjust
exercises.note: Add reps or weight wherever lifts felt easy

meals.name: Breakfast
meals.tip: Eat within 30 min of waking. Eggs are a complete protein source with healthy fats.

meals.name: Mid-Morning Snack
meals.tip: High protein + healthy fats — keeps you anabolic between meals.

meals.name: Lunch
meals.tip: White rice digests fast and replenishes muscle glycogen effectively.

meals.name: Pre-Workout Snack
meals.tip: Eat 45–60 min before training. Carbs = fuel. Protein = muscle primer.

meals.name: Post-Workout Meal
items: 220g lean ground beef or canned tuna
meals.tip: Eat within 60 min post-training. Most important meal for muscle growth.

meals.name: Dinner
meals.tip: Salmon provides omega-3s that reduce inflammation and support recovery.

meals.name: Night Snack
items: 1 tbsp honey or a handful of berries
meals.tip: Cottage cheese = slow-digesting casein protein. Feeds muscles overnight.

tips.title: How to Use These Weights
tips.body: These are suggested starting weights for a 67 kg beginner. They are intentionally conservative. If a weight feels too easy from rep 1, go one step heavier. If you can't hit the bottom of the rep range with good form, go lighter. Ego has no place in week 1.

tips.title: When to Increase Weight
tips.body: When you can complete ALL sets at the TOP of the rep range with clean form, add 2–2.5 kg to dumbbell moves and 2.5–5 kg to barbell moves next session. This is progressive overload — the engine of muscle growth.

tips.title: Run Slow to Run Better
tips.body: 80% of your jogging should feel easy — conversational pace. Most beginners run too fast and burn out. Slow down, and you'll run longer, recover faster, and actually enjoy it.

tips.title: Cardio & Lifting Schedule
tips.body: Cardio sits on Wednesday (easy jog) and Saturday (long jog) — away from leg days to minimize interference. Never do heavy cardio right before or after a leg session.

tips.title: Fuel Your Cardio Days
tips.body: On jog days, add an extra banana or a slice of toast to your pre-workout snack. Cardio burns extra glycogen — you need those carbs, especially while bulking.

tips.title: The Gada Advantage
tips.body: The Indian gada builds shoulder health, rotational core strength, and grip that most gym-goers never develop. Train it on Wednesdays as active recovery — it enhances your pressing and pulling strength over time.

tips.title: Sleep is Non-Negotiable
tips.body: Aim for 8–9 hours per night. Muscle is built during rest. Adding cardio means your body needs even more recovery — don't cut sleep to fit in extra training.
